observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations to the Municipal Mayor to require the:
1) Inventory Committee to:
a) Prepare RPCPPE in accordance with the format prescribed by existing
regulations;
b) See to it that RPCPPE per category reconciled with balances of PPEs in
the SFPos prepared from the records of the Accounting Office;
2) The General Services Officer to initiate disposal of unserviceable properties in
order to dropped these accounts in the books after proper disposal procedures
are undertaken in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
3) City Accountant to:
a) Provide depreciation on all depreciable assets;
b) Support all de-recognition of PPEs with the required supporting
documents before dropping the accounts from the books of accounts;
c) Reclassify items with unit cost of P15,000 below to inventories

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. Municipal Engineer to provide the local accountant and the general
services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects;
2. Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Voucher to record the
beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the
General Ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D),
respectively;
3. General Services Officer to:
a. maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
b. keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads
and its components.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to submit all supporting documents to validate recorded
transaction in the General Journal affecting the Cash in Bank account and
henceforth, submit all supporting documents of recorded financial
transactions in the General Journals.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to submit all supporting documents on all transactions recorded
in the General Journal to include the JEVs, Contracts/POs, Purchase
Request, Notice of Award, and or Delivery Receipts recognized as Accounts
Payable and the JEVs together with the SSMI and RIS taken up in the
books, the expense portion of the inventories, in accordance with existing
laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal
Development Council, require the:
1. Municipal Accounting Office to maintain separate books of accounts for
the 20% DF in accordance with regulations;
2. The Municipal Budget Officer to render a periodic update to the
Municipal Mayor on the Status of Appropriation, Allotment and
Obligations of the 20% DF and identify excess funds of completed
projects and recommend the reversion and made available for re-
appropriation;
3. The Municipal Planning and Development Office to ensure the
implementation of PPAs during the year;
4. The Municipal Engineers Office, and, other concerned implementing
offices, to monitor the implementation of PPAs of the 20% Development
Fund, in order to achieve the objectives of the Fund;
5. Require the Municipal Development Council to revisit PPAs charged to
Continuing Appropriations, identify balances to be rechanneled to more
priority and relevant PPAs of the Municipality, and, ensure that the
development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned
and procurement-and implementation-ready in order to achieve the
objectives of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to ensure implementation of
PPAs on fund transferred from other NGAs and coordinate with the SAs to
provide Memorandum of Agreements (MOA) or its equivalent in order to
provide the pertinent information as to the utilization of the fund, and to
require the Municipal Accountant to initiate preparation of final Fund
Utilization Reports and refund unutilized balances to the SAs.

We recommended further that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to recognize utilized fund transfers as income using the Subsidy
from NGAs and stop using the Grants and Donations in Cash.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a.) BAC Chairman to submit the lacking documentary requirements to rectify the
deficiencies noted and henceforth, ensure that required documentation from
contractors or suppliers are complete and in accordance with the existing laws
rules and regulations; and
b.) Municipal Accountant to:
1) prepare an adjusting journal entry to recognize the Medical
Equipment of P39,700.00 as a PPE and reduce the same amount in the
Medical, Dental and Laboratory Inventory; and
2) ensure the completeness and propriety of supporting documents for every
disbursement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the MDRRM
Council, require the:
1) MDRRMO to:
a) Ensure that PPAs programmed for DRRMF should be responsive to
disaster preparedness;
b) Monitor the implementation of PPAs charged to the MDRRMF in
order to ensure readiness of the Municipality during disasters and
report to the MDRRMC Chairman on the status of implementation
of MDRRM PPAs;
c) Include in the Annual MDRRMFIP the STF.
2) Municipal Budget Officer to:
a) Stop the practice of utilizing MDRRMF for the payment of JO
personnel and other PPAs not related to DRRM;
b) Appraise the Municipal Mayor, Chairman of the MDRRMC, on the
utilization of MDRRMF Current Year, Continuing Appropriations,
and STF, to keep him informed on the status of utilization of
MDRRM funds so that necessary action can be taken to satisfactorily
attain the intended purposes and objectives of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to ensure that an Acknowledgment
Receipt be maintained for In-Kind Donations received for DRRM purposes
and require the:
1) Municipal Treasurer to:
a.) Ensure the received donations in cash should be deposited in the
bank account exclusively maintained for the DRRM Fund;
b.) Submit to the Accounting Office daily, a separate Report of
Collections and Deposits (RCD) for DRRM, and other supporting
documents such as Official Receipts and deposit slips pertaining to
the donations in cash received, for recording in the books of accounts
of the Municipality.
2) Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain separate SL for cash
donations below 	6100,000.00 labeled as Various Donors per purpose,
as required in Item IV.B.7 of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15,
2014;
3) The General Service Officer to prepare and maintain:
a. Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations-DRRM
b. Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM
c. Inventory List of all procured and donated items
4) The MSWDO to submit the Distribution List duly acknowledged by the
recipients.

c.) the conduct of physical count of the inventories under the Trust Fund books to ascertain its existence.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the following: a.) the Property Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to follow strictly Section 189 of GAM for LGUs by preparing the RIS and RSMI; and b.) with the absence of the LGU website, utilize instead its facebook account and make the necessary posting as part of their public disclosure requirements.

a.) Implemented
b.) Implemented

observations.recommendation: We commended Management for its full compliance on the receipt and timely distribution of the subsidies to the identified eligible families within the Municipality, also for submitting the liquidation reports within the required time to the DSWD.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor, the Department Heads involved in project implementation should adopt a stricter measure to ensure delivery or completion of projects, such as: 1) for projects with negative slippages of more than 10%, rescind the contract, forfeit the performance guaranty and allow other contractors to come in and bring the project to completion as allowed under Paragraph 8.4 of R.A. 9184 IRR; 2) if negative slippage reaches 15% or more, initiate the blacklisting procedures as prescribed under GPPB Resolution No. 40-2017.
observations.reason: Instead of processing payment of delayed projects, withhold the money retained and reserve the same for payment of repairs for defects and liabilities discovered before final acceptance in case the contractor refuses to oblige.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor adopt a stricter measure to ensure delivery or completion of projects, such as: (1) for projects with negative slippages of more than 10%, rescind the contract, forfeit the performance guaranty and allow other contractors to come in and bring the project to completion as allowed under Paragraph 8.4 of R.A. 9184 IRR; (2) if negative slippage reaches 15% or more, initiate the blacklisting procedures as prescribed under GPPB Resolution No. 40-2017.
observations.reason: Despite efforts of the municipal government, the perfection of the current waste management system has not yet been made. Several project components in the Solid Waste Recycling Facilities have been funded and developments in the Eco Park were made; implementation of ordinance on "No Plastic Days Policy" is being pushed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the different department heads should: (a) optimize its 20% DF approved budget for the attainment of its developmental goals; (b) include in their plans and programs projects that have an impact on health and environmental issues; and (c) continue to be compliant with the provisions of JMC No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.reason: The same audit observation in AAR CY 2021 showed that there were already implemented projects as of date. However, implementation of some grants are still pending due to complexity of project menu.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, MPDC, and Municipal Agricultural Officer fast-track the implementation of priority programs, Competitive Bidding were already conducted but were declared failed. Negotiations were also conducted but no supplier rendered their offer.
observations.reason: There were already projects implemented as of date. However, project implementation of some grants are still pending due to complexity of project menu.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with the members of the Local Sanggunian in coordination with the national government should do some fundamental jobs, such as: (1) Craft ordinances and issuing executive orders related to waste management and environmental protection such as the 'No Plastic Days Policy'; (2) mobile solid waste system including garbage classification, collection and recycling; (3) land fill management; and (4) focus on the recycling methods of the recyclables.
observations.reason: Several project components in the solid waste management system such as garbage classification, collection and recycling are being implemented; the municipality has acquired solid waste system funded by the Provincial Government and developments in the Eco Park were undertaken; implementation of ordinance on 'No Plastic Days Policy' is being pushed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, MPDC, Municipal Agricultural Officer, DSWD and all other offices concerned fast-track the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities to provide constituents of the failed projects. Competitive Bidding were already conducted but were declared failed. Negotiations were conducted but no supplier has rendered their offer.
observations.reason: There were already projects implemented as of date. However, implementation of some grants are still pending due to complexity of project menu.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to request for a write-off on long standing accounts receivable with the Commission on Audit.
We recommended that the Mayor cause the processing of the certificate of title of the property purchased for the Evacuation Center.
observations.reason: On-going implementation of processing the certificate of title of the property purchased for the Evacuation Center.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the Human Resource Management Officer to adhere to the provisions of Section 77 of RA No. 7160. Further, to fill-up the vacant position per plantilla items/position of the municipality to handle position works that require regular employees.

We recommended that the Municipal BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, MPDC, Agricultural Officer and all other offices concerned to fast-track the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities to provide benefits due them.
observations.reason: Project implementation of some grants are still pending due to complexity of project menu. Competitive bidding were conducted but declared failed. Negotiations were also conducted but no supplier has rendered their offer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended such as: (1) Plan Crafting and issuing ordinances and executive orders related to waste management and environmental protection such as mobile solid waste system of the Municipality, including, garbage collection and recycling; (2) Adding more recycling facilities; (3) Focusing on the recycling methods of the recyclables; and (4) Landfill management.
observations.reason: Partially implemented. The LGU already acquired mobile solid waste system funded by the Provincial Government; and several project components in the Solid Waste Management developments in the Eco Park including, garbage collection, Material Recycling Facilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, MPDC, Agricultural Officer, DSWD and all other offices concerned to fast-track the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities to provide benefits due them.
observations.reason: Partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to request for a write-off.
observations.reason: The LGU already submitted to COA the request for write-off. No action/decision yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the allocation of funds to process the certificate of title of the property purchased for the Evacuation Center.
observations.reason: The LCE designated a personnel to facilitate the transfer the Certificate of Title. But, as of the moment, the processing of the Certificate of Title is still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management Officer to adhere to the provisions of Section 77 of RA No. 7160. Further, we also recommend the filling up of the vacant plantilla position to handle position works that require career regular employees.
observations.reason: The LGU has no choice left but to hire job orders to augment the workforce in the delivery of public service. Nevertheless, the municipality is recommended to fill-up the vacant plantilla items/position to handle position works that require career regular employees.

observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee
and:
a. require the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of all the
local roads under its jurisdiction, prepare and submit a verified
Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System (RPCRNS).
b. direct the General Services Officer, Municipal Engineer and Municipal
Accountant to reconcile records between offices; and
c. require all officers concerned to strictly comply to the accounting and
reporting guidelines on the Local Road Asset Management System
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

2.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile its
records and determine the causes of the variance and proper
disposition thereof; and
2. Direct the Property Officer to maintain property cards with complete
historical data.

3.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to update its
Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards respectively;
b. the Municipal Treasurer to prepare monthly RSMI and the Municipal
Accountant to record the corresponding JV; and
c. the conduct of physical count of inventories under the Trust Fund
books to ascertain its existence.

4.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the
following:
a. the Property Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to follow
strictly Section 189 of GAM for LGUs by preparing the RIS and
RSMI; and
b. with the absence of LGU website, utilize instead its official
facebook account and make the necessary posting as part of their
compliance to public disclosure requirements.

6.12 We recommended that the Mayor to direct the Accountant and the
Treasurer to refrain from processing of payment for the release of
retention money for the delayed projects. Instead, withhold the money
retained and reserve the same for payment of repairs for defects and
liabilities discovered before final acceptance in case the contractor refuses
to oblige.

7.14 We recommended that the Mayor, with the Department Heads involved in
project implementation adopt a stricter measure to ensure timely delivery
or completion of projects, such as:
1. For projects with negative slippages of more than 10%, rescind the
contract, forfeit the performance guaranty and allow other
contractors to come in and bring the project to completion as allowed
under Paragraph 8.4 of the R.A. 9184 IRR;
2. If negative slippage reaches 15% or more, initiate the blacklisting
procedures as prescribed under GPPB Resolution No. 40-2017.

8.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the different
department heads should:
1. optimize its 20% DF approved budget for the attainment of its
developmental goals; and
2. include in their plans and programs projects that have an impact on
health and environmental issues; and (c) to continue to be complaint
with the provisions of JMC No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to: Observe the proper accounting of borrowing costs in accordance with IPSAS No. 5; and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct noted deficiency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to institute measures for the immediate and proper disposal of all obsolete and unserviceable properties as stipulated in Section 379 of RA 7160.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor: (1) to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the mayor or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their cash advance; (2) not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash advance is settled; (3) to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period after being sent notice and demand; (4) to furnish all department head listings of all unliquidated cash advances of employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect liquidation.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to issue an executive Order creating the Oversight Committee which will review the current Revenue Code of the Municipality and recommend revisions thereon for submission to the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to cause the maximization and optimal utilization of the 20% DF allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of its social, economic and environmental development.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive Mayor to direct the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the required reports pursuant to RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the GAD Focal Person to establish and maintain a GAD Database of the LGU base on Annex B of the MCW-IRR.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider the filling up of plantilla position to perform the duties currently performed by JO workers and cause the charging of the wages of job order workers against the MOOE of local projects authorized by the Local Sangguniang Bayan contrary to Section 77 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to evaluate closely each request for contract time extensions. Refrain from giving time extensions based on mere ordinary unfavorable weather conditions. Aside from the PAG-ASA rainfall data, require other corroborating evidence such as engineer’s logbook or construction foreman’s logbook to establish unworkability of the days applied for extension.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to include as a requirement for contract extensions, the consent of each bondsman. This way, full performance of the projects based on approved plans and specifications is still secured even on the extended periods.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to adapt monitoring mechanism to detect variances in the amount to be remitted and withheld.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk and Management Officer to fully implement the planned programs, activities and projects for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery so that the MDRRM Fund would be utilized and the intended benefits would be fully achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management Officer to adhere to the provisions of Section 77 of RA No. 7160 and recommend filling-up the vacant plantilla position of the municipality to handle position works that require career regular service.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has left no choice but to hire job orders to augment workforce in the deliverance of the service.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. observe the proper accounting of borrowing costs in accordance with IPSAS
No. 5 and;
b. prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct noted deficiency.

2.9 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to
institute measures for the immediate and proper disposal of all obsolete and
unserviceable properties as stipulated in Section 379 of RA 7160.

3.9 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant:
a. to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period
a written reminder under signature of the mayor or his duly authorized
representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their
cash advance;
b. not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash
advance is settled;
c. to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and
employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed
period after being sent notice and demand.
d. to furnish all department head listings of all unliquidated cash advances of
employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect liquidation.

4.13 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to issue an Executive Order creating
an Oversight Committee in compliance to DILG- DOF JMC No. 2019-01 dated
May 17, 2019, which will review the current Revenue Code of the Municipality
and recommend revisions thereon for submission to the Sangguniang Bayan.

5.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to cause the maximization and
optimal utilization of the 20% DF allocated for the implementation of various
projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of
its social, economic and environmental development.

6.10 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the MDRRMO to prepare
and submit the required reports pursuant to RA No. 10121 and COA Circular
No. 2012-002.

7.6 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the BAC Chairman,
Municipal Engineer, MPDC, and Municipal Agricultural Officer to fast-track the
implementation of priority programs, projects and activities to provide
constituents of the benefits due them.

8.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the GAD Focal Person to
establish and maintain a GAD Database of the LGU base on Annex B of the
MCW-IRR.

9.9 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to cause the charging of wages of
job order workers against the MOOE of local projects authorized by the Local
Sanggunian and not against the MOOE of any office pursuant to Section 77 of
RA No. 7160 and consider the filling up of unfilled plantilla position to perform
the duties currently performed by JO workers.

10.7 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
a) issue executive orders related to waste management and environmental
protection such as “No plastic days policy”;
b) perfect the current waste management system of the Municipality, including,
garbage classification, collection and recycling;
c) add more waste recycling facilities; and,
d) focus on the recycling methods of the recyclables.

11.14 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Engineer
to evaluate closely each request for contract time extensions. Refrain from
giving time extensions based on mere ordinary unfavorable weather
conditions. Aside from the PAG-ASA rainfall data, require other corroborating
evidence such as engineer’s logbook or construction foreman’s logbook to
establish unworkability of the days applied for extension.

12.6 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer to
include as a requirement for contract extensions, the consent of each
bondsman. This way, full performance of the projects based on approved plans
and specifications is still secured even on the extended periods.

13.8 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to
adapt monitoring mechanism to detect variances in the amount to be remitted
and withheld.

We recommended for the adoption of a decentralized solid waste management system by providing financial and technical assistance for the establishment operation of MRFs in the 6 barangays. We likewise recommended for a wide dissemination of information on source reduction, recycling and composting programs in conformance to the provisions of Section 32 and Section 12 respectively, of the IRR of RA 9003.
We enjoined the local officials for the utilization of the unexpended prior year’s balances amounting to Pl,410,928.0l.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has appropriated under the 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the SWM Board, together with the municipality’s local officials to implement the approved the SWP Plan for a safe environment so as to promote the tourism activities in the Island of Limasawa as the being a tourist destination, at
observations.managementAction: The LGU has implemented the SWP Plan under the 2017 20% of IRA Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exert extra effort to prove the existence of the above cited accounts and if effort proved futile, a resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan should be passed to apply for the write-off which should only be done upon prior authority from COA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the inventory committee to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) to COA; b. Compel the General Services Officer/Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the unreconciled composition of the records between the Accounting office and General Services office totaling ₱10,947,705.51.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to fully implement the planned programs, activities and projects for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery so that the MDRRM Fund would be utilized and the intended benefits would be fully achieved.
observations.reason: The LGU has experienced delays in the procurement process and there were several election ban in 2019 that resulted to a halt in the procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement the following measures: 1. Require the Municipal Engineer to conduct complete and detailed inspection before projects are certified complete. 2. Create an Inspection Committee which shall be tasked to conduct joint inspection on all projects as a requirement before projects are paid.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has experienced delays in the procurement process and during implementation stage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, MPDC, Municipal Agricultural Officer, DSWD and all other offices concerned to fast-track the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities to provide constituents of the benefits due them.
observations.reason: The LGU experienced delays in the procurement process and there were several election ban in 2019 that resulted to a halt in the procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to request for a write off with the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: The LGU is in the process of dropping the long overdue account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to prepare the required voucher in all loan repayments. Likewise, the ADA and bank statement should also be attached as supporting documents to the updated bank disbursement voucher.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Treasurer to intensify their collections and train additional personnel to take charge of delinquent accounts.
To ensure the transfer of the certificate of title of the property purchased for the Evacuation Center.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has designated a personnel to process the certificate of title. But, as of the moment, the processing of the transfer of Certificate of Title is still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Focal Person to develop a well-designed, current and recalibrated community awareness program and choose the appropriate means of communication for it to be effective.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Focal Person to maintain disposal site records in the Residual Containment Area.

We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type of waste should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist in developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) cause the allocation of funds to purchase the appropriate safety clothing as prescribed in the Manual for Operation and Maintenance at Sanitary Landfill; b) direct the Solid Waste Management Focal Person to ensure that any person entering the RCA must wear the proper safety clothing that the standards and processes prescribed in the manual are complied with so that the operations at the RCA are effective and safe.

We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a secured gate at the entrance to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers on the RCA. This will also help address the occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit on time to the COA Audit Team the Accomplishment Report as well as the GAD Plan and Budget.
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate/provide funds for the implementation of Programs/Projects/Activities contained in its Annual Gender and Development Plan.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to establish and maintain a GAD Database where processed consolidated sex-disaggregated data will be stored.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit a justification on the incurrence of unauthorized expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records with that of the GSIS and conduct regular reconciliation with the employees’ payroll so that what is deducted from the employees’ salaries should be the same amount to be remitted to the GSIS on or before their due dates.

Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to submit the accounts of the Municipality regularly on every 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise all accountable officers are to turn-over to the accounting division for recording purposes reports as well as source documents within five (5) days after the end of each month.

1.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) Require the inventory committee to
submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE)
to COA; and (b) Compel the General Services Officer/Supply Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the
PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account
balance.

2.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund Officer to fully implement the planned programs, activities and
projects for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery so that the
MDRRM Fund would be utilized and the intended benefits would be fully achieved.

3.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement the following measures:
1. Require the Municipal Engineer to conduct complete and detailed inspection before
projects are certified complete.
2. Create an Inspection Committee which shall be tasked to conduct joint
inspection on all projects as a requirement before projects are paid.

4.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman, Municipal
Engineer, MPDC, Municipal Agricultural Officer, DSWD and all other offices concerned
to fast-track the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities to provide
constituents of the benefits due them.

5.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to request
for a write off on long standing accounts receivable with the Commission on Audit.

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountant and Treasurer to
prepare a disbursement voucher in all loan repayments. Likewise, the ADA and bank
statement should also be attached as supporting documents to the disbursement
voucher.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Treasurer to intensify
their RPT collections and train additional personnel to take charge of delinquent
accounts.

8.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the allocation of funds to process the
certificate of title of the property purchased for the Evacuation Center.

9.12 To ensure effective implementation of the solid waste management plan, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management (SWM)
Focal Person to develop a well-designed, current and recalibrated community
awareness program and choose the appropriate means of communication for it to be
effective.

10.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Focal
Person to maintain disposal site records in the Residual Containment Area (RCA).

11.6 We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type of waste
should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist in developing
effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste management.

12.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) cause the allocation of funds to purchase the appropriate safety clothing as
prescribed in the Manual for Operation and Maintenance at Sanitary Landfill;
b) direct the Solid Waste Management Focal Person should ensure that any person
entering the RCA must wear the proper safety clothing that the standards and
processes prescribed in the manual are complied with that the operations at the
RCA are effective and safe.

13.5 We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a secured gate at the entrance to
minimize illegal visitors or scavengers on the RCA. This will also help address the
occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.

14.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management
Officer to adhere to the provisions of Section 77 of RA No. 7160. Further, we also
recommend fill-up the vacant position per plantilla items/position of the municipality to
handle position works that require career regular employees.

15.5 We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit on time to the COA Audit Team
the Accomplishment Report as well as the GAD Plan and Budget.

16.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to
allocate/provide funds for the implementation of Programs/Projects/Activities contained
in its Annual Gender and Development Plan.

17.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to establish a
GAD Database where processed consolidated sex-disaggregated data will be stored.
Maintenance of these data shall be done through regular updating and checking in terms
of improvement of status or changes in the condition of men and women in the locality.

18.5 We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit a justification on the incurrence of
unauthorized expenditures.

19.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile
their records with that of the GSIS. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall also
conduct regular reconciliation with the employees’ payroll so that what is deducted from
the employees’ salaries should be the same amount to be remitted to the GSIS on or
before their due dates.

20.9 Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommended that the
Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to submit the accounts of the Municipality
regularly on every 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise all accountable officers
are to turn-over to the accounting division for recording purposes reports as well as
source documents within five (5) days after the end of each month.
a) Require the inventory team to complete without delay the conduct of physical inventory of inventory.
observations.managementAction: 2016 AAR The Municipal Mayor reorganized the composition of the Inventory Committee, instructing the conduct of physical count. The new team, led by Mr.Jeremias R. Tumamak, conducted the physical count, except for Road Networks & CIPs which to be recorded 100% in books by 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive secure the MOA with the contracting party to include a provision specifying the duties and responsibilities of the funding agency, the municipality and the third-party beneficiaries. The MOA must also include a work and financial plan that would identify and address all details of the project.
observations.managementAction: 2016 AAR The Local officials appropriated the unexpended prior years' balances in the regional offices for funds granted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the establishment of MRF under the 20% budget and wide dissemination of information on source reduction, recycling and composting programs in conformance to the provisions of Section 32 and Section 12 respectively, of the IRR of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR The LGU has appropriated 20% budget. No appropriation for barangay functional MRFs due to lack of funds. The LGU prioritized the establishment of ECO Park and MRF.
observations.reason: Projects were not implemented due to lack of funds and difficulty in accessing the site.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair of the Local School Board, strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of the LGC, Article 363 of Implementing Rules and Item 4 of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 of the DECS, DBM and DILG, or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of the Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: 2017 SEF complied the utilization of the Special Education Fund strictly on programs set forth in the DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 1998 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: We enjoined the local officials for the utilization of the unexpended prior years' balances to include the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF of the previous years which were to be transferred to the LDRRMF and include the activities to be funded from the unexpended balances.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR The LGU appropriated the unexpended balances for disaster risk reduction and management program, including box culvert at Brgy. Magallanes; however, appropriation was halted due to procurement and appraisal issues.
observations.reason: Projects were not implemented due to procurement difficulties and accessing the site; the LGU has to appropriate the unexpended prior year balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents attached before submission to the COA Office and see to it that compliance with the above mentioned documents is adhered to.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR The Municipal complied with the requirements and the cited documents as part of the processing of payments. The LGU has complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the constitution of an Inventory Committee composed of the Municipal Treasurer and/or her representative and two (2) management officials to be responsible in conducting the physical count of the recorded property, plant and equipment; prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); maintain property ledger cards and property cards; and periodically conduct reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor reorganized the composition of the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count in 2015; the new team conducted the physical count, except for Road Networks & CIPs which to be recorded 100% in books by 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the SWM Board, together with the municipality’s officials to implement the SWM Plan and implement the SWP Plan for a safe environment to promote tourism activities in the Island of Limasawa.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR The LGU appropriated 20% of IRA Budget; consequently the LGU will generate more revenues. No utilization for 2017 SWMP due to difficulty accessing the site.
observations.reason: No utilization for 2017 SWMP due to difficulty in accessing the site.

observations.recommendation: We recommend: 1. Instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of PPE’s within a specific time frame 2015; 2. For the Inventory Team to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); 3. Require the Accounting Office and Property Officer to maintain the property ledger cards and property cards, respectively; and 4. Instruct the Accountant and Property Officer to periodically conduct a reconciliation of their property records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor reorganized the composition of the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count; the new team conducted the physical count except for Road Networks & CIPs which to be recorded 100% in books by 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should exert extra effort to prove the existence of the accounts and if effort proved futile, a claims resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan should be passed to apply for the write-off of the accounts which should only be done upon prior authority from COA.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 AAR Not complying the requirements for dropping the accounts. LGU is on the process of complying the requirements for dropping the accounts.
observations.reason: LGU is on the process of complying the requirements for dropping the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

e) Compel the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the requirements
of law and rules on cash advances specifically concerning grant of new
ones only if the previously given are already settled.

f) For those cash advances which remained unliquidated for more than
60 days as of December 31,2017, the Municipal Accountant shall
demand for the liquidation from the concerned accountable persons
including the suspension of salaries, if warranted.

g) Direct the Municipal Accountant to update regularly the subsidiary
ledgers for all cash advances, and

h) Direct the Municipal Accountant to have a complete file of
disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents for all
unliquidated cash advances, by labeling it as “Active Cash Advances”,
which will support the subsidiary ledgers and the aging of cash
advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant to exhaust all possible remedies for purposes of causing the
liquidation of transferred funds to concerned recipients. This could be initiated by
the sending of letters to defaulting persons/entities demanding for submission of
necessary documents proving the utilization or non-use of transferred funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents attached before
submission to the COA Office and see to it that compliance with the above
mentioned circular be adhered to.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from issuing Certificates
of Final acceptance within the Defects Liability Period. Reserve the issuance only
after one year has expired after the project was certified complete.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretary to post all
Notices of Award, Approved Contract and Notice to proceed in the PhilGEPS
website as required under Section 37.1.6, Section 37.4.2 and Section 54.3 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Budget Officer to allocate 1% of IRA for LCPC plans and programs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all the Department Heads
assigned to spearhead the implementation of these projects to exhaust remedies to
fully implement these projects in a timely manner.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to
maximize the utilization of the MDRRMF in order to achieve the
objectives of the fund and will ensure readiness of the LGU in times of
calamity;
b. Compel the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to
conduct a case study for financial assistance extended to victims of
calamity.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Human Resource
Management Officer to review the duties and functions of the JOs so as not to
duplicate the duties and functions of the regular plantilla employees, and to compel
the Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order workers in compliance
to regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste
Management focal person to prioritize the implementation of programs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD council
together with the LPDO to come-up with the database in attuning to the demands of
GAD issues and concerns.

a) Impose sanctions on each member of the inventory committee who refuse to cooperate and follow his order.
b) Require the inventory team to prioritize and complete without delay the conduct of physical inventory of the LGU's PPE as required by the cited rules. If records are no longer available to provide a basis for the costing of real assets, assessed valuation may be utilized as a reasonable basis for its valuation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor reorganized the composition of the Inventory and Disposal Committee, instructing the conduct of physical count. The new team, led by Mr. Jeremias R. Tumamak, conducted the physical count, except for Road Networks & CIPs which to be recorded 100% in books by 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all fund transfers received in the regional offices for funds granted must have a memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the contracting party to include a provision specifying the duties and responsibilities of the funding agency, the municipality and the third-party beneficiaries. The MOA must also include a work and financial plan that would identify and address all details of the project.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has appropriated the transferred funds granted in the regional offices for the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the construction of MRF under the 20% EDF and wide dissemination of information on source reduction, recycling and composting programs in conformance to the provisions of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: The LGU appropriated the construction of MRF under the 2017 20% budget. The prioritization and planning for MRF was undertaken; however the project was halted due to difficulty in accessing the site. No appropriation for the barangay MRF due to lack of funds. The LGU prioritized establishment of ECO Park and MRF.
observations.reason: Projects were not implemented due to difficulty in accessing the site and lack of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair of the Local School Board, observe the provisions of Section 272 of the LGC, Article 363 of the Implementing Rules and Item 4 of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 of the DECS, DBM and DILG, in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor complied the recommendation and instructed that SEF expenditures be utilized strictly on programs set forth in the DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 1998 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: We enjoined the local officials for the utilization of the unexpended prior years' balances and include the activities to be funded by the unexpended QRF of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund in the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The LGU appropriated the balances for management program, project & activities and included the counterpart fund of the balance for the construction of the subject lot. The LGU prioritized acquisition of land for the expansion of the Evacuation Center.
observations.reason: Projects were appropriated but implementation pending due to procurement and appraisal of the subject lot.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents attached before submission to the COA Office and see to it that compliance with the above mentioned Circulars be adhered to.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied the requirements for completeness of supporting documents as attached in the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the constitution of an Inventory Committee composed of the Municipal Treasurer and or/her representative and two (2) management officials that will be responsible in conducting the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards; and periodically conduct reconciliation of property records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor reorganized the composition of the Inventory Committee, instructing the conduct of physical count. The new team, led by Mr. Jeremias R. Tumamak, conducted the physical count, except for Road Networks & CIPs which to be recorded 100% in books by 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Management the Local Chief Executive require the SWM Board, together with the municipality’s officials to implement the SWMP, thus, implement the SWP Plan for a safe environment so as to promote the tourism activities in the Island of Limasawa.
observations.managementAction: The LGU appropriated programs to implement the SWMP for the 2017 20% of the IRA Budget.
observations.reason: No utilization for the 2017 SWMP due to procurement reasons and difficulty in accessing the site.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Local Chief Executive to: 1. Instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of PPE's within a specific time frame; 2. For the Inventory Team to prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); 3. Require the Accounting Office and Property Officer to maintain the property ledger cards and property cards, respectively; and 4. Instruct the Accountant and Property Officer to periodically conduct a reconciliation of their property records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor reorganized the composition of the Inventory Committee, instructing the conduct of physical count. The new team, led by Mr. Jeremias R. Tumamak, conducted the physical count, except for Road Networks & CIPs which to be recorded 100% in books by 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should exert extra effort to prove the existence of the above cited accounts and if effort proved futile, a claims resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan should be passed to apply for the write-off of the accounts which should only be done upon prior authority from COA.
observations.managementAction: Not complying the requirements for dropping the accounts. LGU is on the process of complying the requirements for dropping the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should exert extra effort to prove the existence of the above cited accounts and if effort proved futile, a resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan should be passed to apply for the write-off of the accounts which should only be done upon prior authority from COA.
observations.reason: LGU is on the process of complying the requirements for dropping the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Require the inventory team to prioritize and complete without delay the
conduct of physical inventory of PPEs as required by the cited rules. If records
are no longer available to provide a basis for the costing of real assets,
assessed values reflected in the Real Property Assessments of the Assessor’s
Office may be utilized as a reasonable basis for its valuation.
b) Impose sanctions on each member of the inventory committee who refuse to
cooperate and follow order.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly observe the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and that all fund transfers received
must have a memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the contracting party to
include a provision specifying the duties and responsibilities of the funding
agency, the municipality and the third-party beneficiaries. The MOA must also
include a work and financial plant that would identify and address all details of
the project.

We recommended for the adoption of a decentralized solid waste
management system by providing financial and technical assistance for the
establishment operation of functional and standard-based MRFs in the barangays
of the Municipality.
We likewise recommended for a wide dissemination of information on
source reduction, recycling and composting programs in conformance to the
provisions of the IRR of RA 9003.

We recommended that the Local School Board strictly observe the
provisions of Section 272 of the LGC, Article 363 of its Implementing Rules and
Regulations and Item 4 of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 of the DECS, DBM and
DILG, in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We enjoined the local officials for the utilization of the unexpended
balance for disaster risk reduction and management program, project & activities
and include the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF of the previous
years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents attached
before submission to the COA Office and see to it that compliance with the above
mentioned Circular be adhered to.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the constitution of an Inventory Committee composed of the Municipal Treasurer or/her representative and two (2) management officials that will be responsible in conducting the physical count and prepare the Inventory Report of all Property, Plant and Equipment of the local government unit. The Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January of each year. Updated Property Ledger (PLC) and Property Cards shall be maintained by the municipal bookkeeper and treasurer respectively, and both records should be reconciled regularly.
observations.reason: Few PPE items like land improvements and school buildings were not physically counted yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2011-01 on the appropriation and utilization of 20% IRA for projects that contributes to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) remind the members of the Local School Board to strictly adhere to the prioritized operation expenses identified in Section 100 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly to the provisions of Sections 2.3 and 6.1 of PCW- NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-1 to ensure that mainstreaming of GAD PPAs are maximized, thus, fully addressing the prevailing identified gender concerns for the year in the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor exercise prudence in spending government funds, specifically, a) make representation with the PCL – Southern Leyte Federation that seminars initiated by them be held within the province minimize the cost in attending the said activities. Otherwise, request PCL Chapter to at least subsidize the expenses of the participants coming from the province attending the out-of-town seminars called by them; b0 send only participants to seminar/workshop who are directly concern with the subject of the conference called for.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure the faithful compliance with COA Circular No. 75-6 for effective control on the use and maintenance of the LGU motor vehicles, and on fuel, gasoline and oil expenses, thereby preventing wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures on petroleum products utilization.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Health Officer to certify on the purchase request that the drug products being requisitioned/purchased are within and conform to PNDF Essential List, Volume I as required under Executive order No. 49. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should see to it that the DVs and its supporting documents are legitimate, completely filled-up and should be properly completely filled-up.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the SWM Board, together with the municipality’s local officials to immediately implement faithfully the SWP Plan for a safe environment so as to promote the tourism activities in the Island of Limasawa as the cradle of Christianity in the Far East.
observations.reason: There were few implementations of the SWM Plan due to lack of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to reconcile their records (the cashbook and the subsidiary/general ledger) monthly as possible or at least at the end of each quarter. The Municipal Treasurer shall total, foot, rule and close the books at the end of each month and prepare monthly certification in the cashbook of her total monthly cash accountability.

We recommend for Local Chief Executive (LCE) to- 1. Instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of PPE’s within a specific time frame this 2015. 2. For the Inventory Team to prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). 3. Require the Accounting Office and Property Officer to maintain the property ledger cards and property cards, respectively; and 4. Instruct the Accountant and Property Officer to periodically conduct a reconciliation of their property records.
observations.reason: Few PPE items like land improvements and school buildings were not physically counted yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the faithful compliance with COA Circular No. 75-6 for effective control on the use and maintenance of the LGU motor vehicles of fuel, gasoline and oil expenses, thereby preventing wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures on fuel consumption.
